title: Separate background outputs into dedicated UI section + explicit process-log actor labels
summary: Introduce structured background-result sections and explicit actor/source labels so main-agent output, background task output, and background agent output are visually distinct in both final responses and live process logs.
details:
## Why this change
Current behavior blends background output into the same plain-text assistant message, which makes provenance unclear.

Source evidence:
- `src/gateway/routes/chat.router.ts` appends background join output directly into `finalText` as plain text (`Background results:` lines) at ~1904-1924, so UI receives one merged message body.
- `src/gateway/routes/chat.router.ts` emits generic `tool_call`/`tool_result` events without explicit actor-type metadata at ~2209 and ~2514.
- Console/process log prefixes still use `[v2]` in key runtime paths (e.g., `chat.router.ts`, `core/server.ts`, `subagent-executor.ts`), which hides user-facing identity intent (Prom vs background entities).
- Background runtime emits task activity (`task_tool_call`, `task_panel_update`) in `src/gateway/tasks/background-task-runner.ts` (~507-531), but interactive/main-chat process attribution for spawned ephemeral background runs is not first-class.

## Exact source edits
- `src/gateway/routes/chat.router.ts`
  - Replace plain-text background join append behavior with structured response sections so background outcomes are emitted as a dedicated section in `done.sections` (e.g., `type: "background_results"`) instead of concatenated into one blob.
  - Add per-event actor metadata in SSE emissions for main interactive tool execution, e.g. `actor_label: "Prom"`, `actor_type: "main_agent"`, `source_kind: "interactive"`.
  - Emit explicit lifecycle SSE events when background IDs are spawned/joined (for process log clarity): `background_spawned`, `background_joined`, `background_pending`, `background_failed`.
  - Keep backward compatibility by preserving existing text fallback for clients not yet rendering structured section cards.
- `src/gateway/tasks/background-task-runner.ts`
  - Include actor metadata on forwarded task events (`task_tool_call`, `task_panel_update`, `task_step_done`) with `actor_label: "Background Task"`, `actor_type: "background_task"`.
- `src/gateway/teams/team-dispatch-runtime.ts`
  - Add explicit actor metadata when dispatching/relaying subagent background activity: `actor_label: "Background Agent"`, `actor_type: "background_agent"`.
- `src/gateway/agents-runtime/subagent-executor.ts`
  - For `background_spawn/background_status/background_progress/background_join` tool responses, add deterministic metadata fields (`kind`, `actor_type`) in JSON payloads so UI and logs can render source chips reliably.
  - Update log prefixes from `[v2]` to `[Prom]` where these paths report runtime actions.
- `src/gateway/core/server.ts`
  - Rename WS lifecycle log prefixes from `[v2]` to `[Prom]` for consistent process-log branding.

## Deterministic behavior after patch
- Final assistant output remains readable text, but background outcomes are always available as a separate structured section payload for UI rendering in a distinct panel.
- Process log entries carry deterministic actor/source metadata:
  - Main chat agent => `Prom`
  - Long-running task runner => `Background Task`
  - Spawned subagent execution => `Background Agent`
- Existing consumers that only read text continue to function (compat fallback retained).
- No behavior change to scheduling, execution ordering, or join policy semantics.

## Acceptance tests
1. Build/typecheck:
   - Run `npm run build` with no TS errors.
2. Background merge UI contract:
   - In a normal chat session, call `background_spawn` then continue with foreground tools.
   - On completion, verify SSE `done` payload includes a dedicated background-results section object (not only a single merged text body).
3. Process log labels:
   - During foreground execution, verify events include `actor_label: "Prom"`.
   - During a running background task, verify task events include `actor_label: "Background Task"`.
   - During team/subagent background execution, verify relayed events include `actor_label: "Background Agent"`.
4. Backward compatibility:
   - Confirm final textual reply is still populated and non-empty for legacy rendering paths.

## Risks and compatibility
- Risk: Frontend may ignore new section/event metadata until UI render mapping is updated.
  - Mitigation: Keep textual fallback unchanged; additive payload fields only.
- Risk: Event schema drift for consumers expecting bare `tool_call/tool_result`.
  - Mitigation: Do not remove existing fields; only add new metadata keys.
- Compatibility: Additive changes; no breaking API removals.

affectedFiles.description: Emit structured background-results section + actor metadata in SSE and done payloads
Attach background-task actor labels to task event broadcasts
Attach background-agent actor labels to relayed team/agent task events
Add deterministic metadata to background_* tool JSON outputs and update runtime log prefixes
Rename WS process log prefix from [v2] to [Prom]

estimatedImpact: Clear provenance in outputs/process logs; improves trust/debuggability; enables clean UI separation of main vs background execution.
executorPrompt: Execute this approved src patch exactly as specified. 1) Read each affected file with read_source and confirm current flow. 2) Apply only the listed edits to add structured background result sections and actor labels, while preserving compatibility text paths. 3) Ensure process log prefixes are updated from [v2] to [Prom] only in targeted runtime files. 4) Run npm run build; if it fails, stop and report exact error/output. 5) Return a concise change report with exact files changed and validation outcomes.
